name: Gao Xue
title: Wounded Lotus-Robed Cultivator
description: A young cultivator found wounded on the broken trade road, proud enough to hide her name until survival made honesty possible.
relationshipToPlayer: Owes the player a life debt after being treated for three days with crude roadside medicine.
returnHooks: Can repay the life debt with medicine, protection, or information from her family.
Can pull the player into the politics behind the bandit road ambush.
Can recognize whether the player stayed merciful after entering the sect.

name: Old Ren
title: Retired Caravan Guard
description: A cane-carrying former guard whose mortal fighting experience is plain, practical, and hard to impress.
relationshipToPlayer: Tests the player's stance and may become the first person to seriously correct their martial foundation.
returnHooks: Can return as a mortal witness when the player revisits the village.
Can recognize whether the player's cultivation has improved their basics or made them arrogant.
Can introduce caravan-road trouble tied to the wider mortal world.

name: Aunt Lin
title: Village Herbalist
description: A bitter-tongued herbalist who treats medicine as a debt the body must eventually answer.
relationshipToPlayer: Teaches the player that alchemy starts with not poisoning anyone through haste or pride.
returnHooks: Can ask the player to identify a strange immortal herb brought back to the village.
Can become a reason to visit medicine halls or learn safer pill refining.
Can challenge the player if their impurity rises from reckless cultivation.

name: Blacksmith Guo
title: Village Blacksmith
description: A quiet smith who trusts the sound of iron more than claims of talent.
relationshipToPlayer: Teaches the player that reliable tools and reliable bodies are both made through careful repair.
returnHooks: Can repair or upgrade early mortal gear before a dangerous road arc.
Can notice strange properties in low-grade spirit metal.
Can contrast mortal craft with sect refining techniques later.

name: Shrine Keeper Mei
title: Village Shrine Keeper
description: A young shrine keeper with an old stillness, known for counting breath and reading fear before it speaks.
relationshipToPlayer: Teaches breath counting and gives the player's foundation a calmer shape before the mountain trial.
returnHooks: Can sense when the player's breath has changed after sect training.
Can introduce shrine history connected to Cloud essence.
Can become a quiet anchor if the player returns home after violence.

name: Examiner Su
title: Azure Cloud Exam Steward
description: A grey-robed examiner whose brush pauses only when a candidate is unusual enough to deserve a second mark.
relationshipToPlayer: Registers the player for the Azure Cloud exam and later marks the first-trial breakthrough as unusual.
returnHooks: Can remember whether the player passed by endurance, clarity, or character.
Can appear as a bureaucratic gatekeeper for future sect tests.
Can quietly warn the player when elder attention becomes dangerous.

name: Liu Zhen
title: Blue-Silk Liu Clan Heir
description: A polished noble candidate who treats the sect exam like a family entitlement and poverty like a personal insult.
relationshipToPlayer: Mocks the player's village origin, then becomes personally offended when the player survives the first trial and breakthrough.
returnHooks: Can become the first recurring sect rival.
Can use clan influence to make outer sect tasks harder.
Can be forced into grudging respect if repeatedly defeated without cheating.

name: Han Yue
title: Quiet Commoner Candidate
description: A plain-robed candidate who learned from her brother's failed exam and trusts breath more than bravado.
relationshipToPlayer: Warns the player not to let other candidates set their pace before the first trial.
returnHooks: Can become a grounded friend in the outer sect.
Can compare trial results with the player after acceptance.
Can ask for help uncovering what happened during her brother's failed exam.

name: Qiao Min
title: Nervous Merchant Candidate
description: A frightened merchant child with too much purchased advice and not enough confidence in his own feet.
relationshipToPlayer: Receives help before the exam and may become proof that character matters during the hidden trial.
returnHooks: Can become an information source through merchant family letters.
Can repay kindness with supplies or warnings.
Can show how weaker disciples survive through alliances.

name: Senior Brother Wei
title: Outer Sect Guide
description: A patient outer disciple assigned to guide unclaimed candidates through the sect grounds and rules.
personality: more helpful than his bored tone suggests
relationshipToPlayer: Explains the sect's outer rules and becomes the player's first normal senior contact if no elder recruits them early.
returnHooks: Can explain sect rules and contribution systems.
Can warn the player before they offend a hall elder.
Can become a low-pressure senior contact for early outer sect errands.

name: Unnamed Scripture Pavilion Clerk
title: Grey-Robed Clerk
description: A ledger-keeping clerk who guards copied manuals with the bored patience of someone who has seen ambition embarrass itself.
relationshipToPlayer: Marks the player's name after they ask about rules instead of rushing for forbidden manuals.
returnHooks: Can remember whether the player listened to sect rules.
Can gate future scripture access behind reputation or completed tasks.
Can become a low-key source of rumors from the pavilion ledger.

name: Unnamed Medicine Garden Senior Sister
title: Medicine Garden Senior Sister
description: A senior outer disciple who judges new disciples by whether they preserve roots while doing dirty work.
relationshipToPlayer: May notice careful work in the medicine garden and reward patience over speed.
returnHooks: Can offer medicine garden follow-up tasks.
Can become an alchemy contact if the player shows care with herbs.
Can judge the player's impurity and foundation stability later.

name: Elder Shen
title: Outer Sect Bottleneck Elder
description: A patient elder assigned to stop new disciples from mistaking reckless breakthroughs for progress.
relationshipToPlayer: Explains bottleneck preparation after the player survives enough of the outer sect to ask better questions.
returnHooks: Can warn the player when a breakthrough attempt is underprepared.
Can unlock safer bottleneck recipes for disciples who show patience.
Can become a recurring mentor for Mortal Peak and later realm transitions.

name: Unnamed Pine Shadow Senior Disciple
title: Footwork Instructor
description: A senior outer disciple who marks footwork in dust and teaches movement as timing rather than display.
relationshipToPlayer: Introduces Pine Shadow Step and can become a practical martial benchmark.
returnHooks: Can test Pine Shadow Step mastery in the arena.
Can become a friendly rival if the player keeps training footwork.
Can demonstrate the gap between new disciples and experienced outer disciples.

name: Han Qingshi
title: Craft Hall Outer Instructor
description: A square-shouldered Craft Hall instructor who treats every wasted rivet like a moral failing. He teaches basic sect-approved forge work for spirit stones, then better patterns only after a disciple proves useful.
relationshipToPlayer: Offers paid Craft Hall lessons, supervises beginner forge use, and can become a gatekeeper for stronger sect crafting access.
returnHooks: Can discount or waive forge fees once Craft Hall reputation is high.
Can unlock stronger sect-approved weapon and armor patterns after Craft Hall quests.
Can contrast official sect craft discipline with Luo Jiwei's practical town methods.

name: Luo Jiwei
title: Iron Bridge Town Blacksmith
description: A compact man with forearms built from years at the anvil. He taps the iron twice with his knuckle before answering any question, as if the metal has to weigh in first.
relationshipToPlayer: A blacksmith at the trade crossroads who sells reliable tools to whoever can pay without asking what they plan to do with them.
returnHooks: Can upgrade mortal-grade weapons for a spirit stone cost.
Can introduce sect politics from a civilian observer's angle.
Can recognise changes in the player's body bearing as cultivation advances.

name: Shu Yilan
title: Innkeeper, River Lantern Inn
description: A round-faced woman who refills cups before they are empty and remembers every cultivator who has ever passed through her door. She has lived her whole life at the crossroads and knows more about who passes through it than most sects would like.
relationshipToPlayer: A neutral crossroads contact who offers rest, information, and a reminder that the mortal world watches cultivators through complicated eyes.
returnHooks: Can pass new rumours on each visit — hooks for future arcs.
Can ask the player to investigate something on the road.
Can become a safe house contact if sect politics turn dangerous.
